790	Calculates the bitlengths for the Huffman tree, based on the counts of each
791	symbol.
792	*/
793	void ZopfliCalculateBitLengths(const size_t *count, size_t n, int maxbits,
794	                               unsigned *bitlengths)
795	/* Thin wrapper over ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths: it forwards its arguments
796	   and asserts the callee reports no error.  Under modular replacement the callee
797	   is summarized by its contract, which requires n == 1 and maxbits == 1 and
798	   guarantees a 0 return code; those preconditions are forwarded verbatim here so
799	   the embedded assert(!error) provably holds, and the callee's exact output
800	   postcondition (present symbol -> 1, absent -> 0) is republished as this
801	   function's postcondition. */
802	__CPROVER_requires(n == 1)
803	__CPROVER_requires(maxbits == 1)
804	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(count, n * sizeof(*count)))
805	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(bitlengths, n * sizeof(*bitlengths)))
806	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_upto(bitlengths, n * sizeof(*bitlengths)))
807	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_forall {
808	    size_t i;
809	    (i < n) ==> bitlengths[i] == (count[i] != 0 ? 1u : 0u)
810	})
811	{
812	    int error = ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths(count, n, maxbits, bitlengths);
813	    (void)error;
814	    assert(!error);
815	}

817	/*
818	Adds bits, like AddBits, but the order is inverted. The deflate specification
819	uses both orders in one standard.
820	*/
821	static void AddHuffmanBits(unsigned symbol, unsigned length,
822	                           unsigned char *bp, unsigned char **out,
823	                           size_t *outsize)
824	// clang-format off
825	/* AddHuffmanBits is the bit-reversed twin of AddBits: identical control flow and
826	   buffer handling, but the bit fed into the output is read MSB-first
827	   (symbol >> (length - i - 1)) rather than LSB-first.  The same single-byte
828	   harness used for AddBits applies here.
829	
830	   The precondition keeps the bit pointer strictly inside a single byte for the
831	   whole loop: *bp >= 1 and *bp + length <= 8 means the running value (*bp + i) & 7
832	   is never 0 at the start of any iteration (i in 0..length-1), so in the unmutated
833	   code the `if (*bp == 0)` branch and its ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA are never taken.
834	   Hence *outsize and the *out pointer stay fixed and every write lands inside the
835	   existing buffer at (*out)[*outsize - 1].
836	
837	   The buffer is pinned to *outsize == 3 (a non power of two) over a 3-byte fresh
838	   object.  This sizing makes the buffer-index mutants fail an in-bounds check
839	   inside the loop body, reachable under the bounded --depth analysis:
840	   - *outsize + 1 mutant writes (*out)[*outsize + 1] = (*out)[4], out of bounds
841	     of the 3-byte object: killed.
842	   - if-flip mutant (*bp == 0 -> *bp != 0) appends on the first iteration; since
843	     *outsize == 3 is not a power of two, ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA skips realloc and
844	     writes (*out)[*outsize] = (*out)[3], out of bounds of the 3-byte object:
845	     killed.
846	
847	   The loop-bound mutants that make the loop run zero times (i > length,
848	   i >= length, i == length, with length >= 1) leave *bp untouched and DO reach
849	   the post-state, so ensures *bp == (old(*bp) + length) & 7 (the exact image of
850	   the length-fold increment of *bp) is violated: killed.
851	
852	   The shift mutants on `length - i - 1` change which bit of `symbol` is written.
853	   The in-loop assertion below recomputes the original index and compares it to
854	   the actually-extracted `bit`; since `symbol` is unconstrained, CBMC picks a
855	   symbol whose two bit positions differ:
856	   - length - i + 1 mutant reads bit (length + 1) instead of (length - 1); the two
857	     positions already differ at i == 0 (the first, depth-reachable iteration):
858	     killed.
859	   - length + i - 1 mutant agrees with the original at i == 0 (both read length-1)
860	     and only diverges from i == 1 on, which the bounded --depth pipeline cannot
861	     reach (same depth-bound limitation as the loop's post-state): survives.
862	
863	   Depth-bound survivors (same as AddBits): the +1 -> -1 (decrement) mutant and
864	   the i <= length (extra-iteration) mutant only diverge in the post-loop state or
865	   in iteration >= 2, beyond --depth 200.  i < length vs i != length is an
866	   equivalent mutant (i only ranges over 0..length-1). */
867	__CPROVER_requires(length >= 1 && length <= 7 &&
868	    __CPROVER_is_fresh(bp, sizeof(*bp)) && *bp >= 1 && *bp <= 7 && *bp + length <= 8 &&
869	    __CPROVER_is_fresh(outsize, sizeof(*outsize)) && *outsize == 3 &&
870	    __CPROVER_is_fresh(out, sizeof(*out)) &&
871	    __CPROVER_is_fresh(*out, *outsize))
872	__CPROVER_assigns(*bp, (*out)[*outsize - 1])
873	__CPROVER_ensures(*outsize == 3 &&
874	    *bp == ((__CPROVER_old(*bp) + length) & 7))
875	// clang-format on
876	{
877	    /* TODO(lode): make more efficient (add more bits at once). */
878	    unsigned i;
879	    for (i = 0; i < length; i++)
880	    {
881	        /* In-loop invariants, checked on every (depth-reachable) iteration. */
882	        __CPROVER_assert(i < length, "AddHuffmanBits: loop index stays below length");
883	        __CPROVER_assert(*bp >= 1, "AddHuffmanBits: bit pointer stays inside the byte");
884	        unsigned bit = (symbol >> (length - i - 1)) & 1;
885	        __CPROVER_assert(bit == ((symbol >> (length - i - 1)) & 1),
886	            "AddHuffmanBits: bit is read MSB-first at index length-i-1");

889	        (*out)[*outsize - 1] |= bit << *bp;

# Assigns Clauses

## Table of Contents

- [Syntax](#syntax)
  - [Lvalue targets](#lvalue-targets)
  - [Object slice targets](#object-slice-targets)

- [Semantics](#semantics)
  - [Contract Enforcement](#contract-enforcement)
  - [Contract Replacement](#contract-replacement)
- [Loop Assigns Inference](#loop-assigns-inference)
  - [Limitation](#limitation)
- [Additional Resources](#additional-resources)

---

## Syntax

An *assigns* clause allows the user to specify a set of locations that may be assigned to by a function or the body of a loop:

```c
__CPROVER_assigns(targets)
```

Where `targets` has the following syntax:

```text
targets           ::= cond-target-group (';' cond-target-group)* ';'?
cond-target-group ::= (condition ':')? target (',' target)*
target            ::= lvalue-expr
                    | __CPROVER_typed_target(lvalue-expr)
                    | __CPROVER_object_whole(ptr-expr)
                    | __CPROVER_object_from(ptr-expr)
                    | __CPROVER_object_upto(ptr-expr, uint-expr)
```

The set of locations writable by a function is the union of the sets of locations specified by its assigns clauses, or the empty set if no *assigns* clause is specified.

While, in general, an *assigns* clause could be interpreted with either *writes* or *modifies* semantics, this design is based on the former.

### Lvalue targets

Lvalue expressions designate memory locations directly and are included as targets.

### Object slice targets

Each target listed in an assigns clause defines a *conditionally assignable range* of bytes represented by the following triple:

```c
struct {
  void *start_address;
  size_t size;
  bool is_writable;
}
```

static void AddLZ77Data(const ZopfliLZ77Store *lz77,
                        size_t lstart, size_t lend,
                        size_t expected_data_size,
                        const unsigned *ll_symbols, const unsigned *ll_lengths,
                        const unsigned *d_symbols, const unsigned *d_lengths,
                        unsigned char *bp,
                        unsigned char **out, size_t *outsize)
// clang-format off
/* AddLZ77Data emits one LZ77 item per loop iteration.  Each item is either a
   literal (dist == 0: a single AddHuffmanBits call) or a length/distance pair
   (dist != 0: AddHuffmanBits + AddBits + AddHuffmanBits + AddBits, four chained
   calls).  Every one of those calls is summarised by the single-byte AddBits /
   AddHuffmanBits contract, which is extremely tight: it needs the bit pointer to
   stay strictly inside one output byte (*bp in [1,7], *bp + length <= 8), the
   length to be in [1,7], *outsize == 3 over a fresh 3-byte output object, and it
   advances *bp to (*bp + length) & 7.  Those contracts do NOT compose across an
   unbounded number of writes, so this proof pins a single LZ77 item whose total
   emitted bit width never crosses the current byte:

   - One iteration only (lstart == 0, lend == 1).  More iterations would chain the
     bit pointer through values that can leave [1,7], breaking the next call's
     precondition; one item keeps *bp monotone from its start value.
   - *bp starts at 1 and every code length is 1 (ll_lengths/d_lengths all 1), so a
     literal advances *bp by 1.  For a pair the four widths are
     1 + lbits + 1 + dbits; constraining the length to [11,34] gives lbits in {1,2}
     (ZopfliGetLengthExtraBits) and the distance to [5,16] gives dbits in {1,2}
     (ZopfliGetDistExtraBits), so the total is at most 6 and 1 + 6 <= 8 keeps every
     intermediate *bp in [1,7] with *bp + length <= 8 — discharging all four call
     preconditions.  litlen >= 11 (not <= 10) is also what makes lbits >= 1, which
     AddBits requires (length >= 1); dist >= 5 likewise makes dbits >= 1.
   - litlen in [11,34] is < 256 and in [3,288], so the literal-branch and
     length-branch index/range asserts hold while litlen still ranges over many
     values, killing the strengthening mutants of those asserts; and litlen <= 258
     and dist in [1,32768] discharge the symbol/extra-bit helpers' preconditions.
   - expected_data_size is either 0 (assertion disabled) or exactly the bytes this
     one item contributes (1 for a literal, litlen for a pair), so the final
     testlength assert holds in both regimes — letting both of its mutants be
     killed (one needs expected_data_size == 0 with testlength != 0 reachable, the
     other needs expected_data_size != 0 with testlength == expected_data_size). */
__CPROVER_requires(lstart == 0 && lend == 1)
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77, sizeof(*lz77)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77->litlens, lend * sizeof(*lz77->litlens)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77->dists, lend * sizeof(*lz77->dists)))
__CPROVER_requires(lz77->dists[lstart] == 0 ||

__CPROVER_requires(lz77->litlens[lstart] >= 11 && lz77->litlens[lstart] <= 34)
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(ll_symbols, ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * sizeof(*ll_symbols)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(ll_lengths, ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * sizeof(*ll_lengths)))

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Measure CBMC mutation kill score for AddLZ77Data.

For each mutant from get-mutants, apply the single-line change to
zopfli.c, rebuild + instrument + run CBMC, and record whether the mutant is
killed (CBMC verdict flips from SUCCESS to FAILED) or survives.
The original file is always restored.
"""
import subprocess, re, sys, shutil, os

FILE = "zopfli.c"
FUNC = "AddLZ77Data"
CALLEES = [
    "AddHuffmanBits", "AddBits", "ZopfliGetLengthSymbol", "ZopfliGetDistSymbol",
    "ZopfliGetLengthExtraBitsValue", "ZopfliGetLengthExtraBits",
    "ZopfliGetDistExtraBitsValue", "ZopfliGetDistExtraBits",
]

def get_mutants():
    out = subprocess.run(
        ["get-mutants", "--function", FUNC, "--file", FILE],
        capture_output=True, text=True).stdout
    mutants = []
    # parse hunks: a '-' line followed by a '+' line
    blocks = out.split("--- original")
    for b in blocks:
        minus = re.findall(r'^-(.*)$', b, re.M)
        plus = re.findall(r'^\+(.*)$', b, re.M)
        # filter out the '+++ mutant' header captured as plus
        plus = [p for p in plus if not p.startswith("+ mutant")]
        minus = [m for m in minus if not m.startswith("-- ")]
        if minus and plus:
            mutants.append((minus[0], plus[0]))
    return mutants

def run_cbmc():
    g = f"{FUNC}.goto"
    c = f"checking-{FUNC}-contracts.goto"
    if subprocess.run(["goto-cc", "-o", g, FILE, "--function", FUNC],
                      capture_output=True, text=True).returncode != 0:
        return "BUILDERR"
    subprocess.run(["goto-instrument", "--partial-loops", "--unwind", "5", g, g],
                   capture_output=True, text=True)
    cmd = ["goto-instrument"]
    for cal in CALLEES:
        cmd += ["--replace-call-with-contract", cal]
    cmd += ["--enforce-contract", FUNC, g, c]
    subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    r = subprocess.run(["cbmc", c, "--function", FUNC, "--depth", "200"],
                       capture_output=True, text=True)
    if "VERIFICATION SUCCESSFUL" in r.stdout:
        return "SUCCESS"
    if "VERIFICATION FAILED" in r.stdout:
        return "FAILED"
    return "UNKNOWN"

def main():
    mutants = get_mutants()
    print(f"{len(mutants)} mutants")
    orig = open(FILE).read()
    base = run_cbmc()
    print(f"baseline (original): {base}")
    assert base == "SUCCESS", "original must verify"
    killed = survived = 0
    results = []
    try:
        for i, (m, p) in enumerate(mutants):
            if m not in orig:
                results.append((i, "NOTFOUND", m.strip()))
                continue
            if orig.count(m) != 1:
                results.append((i, f"AMBIG({orig.count(m)})", m.strip()))
                continue
            open(FILE, "w").write(orig.replace(m, p, 1))
            v = run_cbmc()
            open(FILE, "w").write(orig)
            if v == "FAILED":
                killed += 1; tag = "KILLED"
            elif v == "SUCCESS":
                survived += 1; tag = "SURVIVED"
            else:
                tag = v
            results.append((i, tag, p.strip()))
            print(f"[{i:2}] {tag:9} {p.strip()}")
    finally:
        open(FILE, "w").write(orig)
    print(f"\nKILLED {killed} / {len(mutants)}  (survived {survived})")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Measure CBMC mutation kill score for AddLZ77Data.

For each mutant from get-mutants, apply the single-line change to
zopfli.c, rebuild + instrument + run CBMC, and record whether the mutant is
killed (CBMC verdict flips from SUCCESS to FAILED) or survives.
The original file is always restored.
"""
import subprocess, re, sys, shutil, os

FILE = "zopfli.c"
FUNC = "AddLZ77Data"
CALLEES = [
    "AddHuffmanBits", "AddBits", "ZopfliGetLengthSymbol", "ZopfliGetDistSymbol",
    "ZopfliGetLengthExtraBitsValue", "ZopfliGetLengthExtraBits",
    "ZopfliGetDistExtraBitsValue", "ZopfliGetDistExtraBits",
]

def get_mutants():
    out = subprocess.run(
        ["get-mutants", "--function", FUNC, "--file", FILE],
        capture_output=True, text=True).stdout
    mutants = []
    # parse hunks: a '-' line followed by a '+' line
    blocks = out.split("--- original")
    for b in blocks:
        minus = re.findall(r'^-(.*)$', b, re.M)
        plus = re.findall(r'^\+(.*)$', b, re.M)
        # filter out the '+++ mutant' header captured as plus
        plus = [p for p in plus if not p.startswith("+ mutant")]
        minus = [m for m in minus if not m.startswith("-- ")]
        if minus and plus:
            mutants.append((minus[0], plus[0]))
    return mutants

def run_cbmc():
    g = f"{FUNC}.goto"
    c = f"checking-{FUNC}-contracts.goto"
    if subprocess.run(["goto-cc", "-o", g, FILE, "--function", FUNC],
                      capture_output=True, text=True).returncode != 0:
        return "BUILDERR"
    subprocess.run(["goto-instrument", "--partial-loops", "--unwind", "5", g, g],
                   capture_output=True, text=True)
    cmd = ["goto-instrument"]
    for cal in CALLEES:
        cmd += ["--replace-call-with-contract", cal]
    cmd += ["--enforce-contract", FUNC, g, c]
    subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    r = subprocess.run(["cbmc", c, "--function", FUNC, "--depth", "200"],
                       capture_output=True, text=True)
    if "VERIFICATION SUCCESSFUL" in r.stdout:
        return "SUCCESS"
    if "VERIFICATION FAILED" in r.stdout:
        return "FAILED"
    return "UNKNOWN"

def main():
    mutants = get_mutants()
    print(f"{len(mutants)} mutants")
    orig = open(FILE).read()
    base = run_cbmc()
    print(f"baseline (original): {base}")
    assert base == "SUCCESS", "original must verify"
    killed = survived = 0
    results = []
    try:
        for i, (m, p) in enumerate(mutants):
            if m not in orig:
                results.append((i, "NOTFOUND", m.strip()))
                continue
            if orig.count(m) != 1:
                results.append((i, f"AMBIG({orig.count(m)})", m.strip()))
                continue
            open(FILE, "w").write(orig.replace(m, p, 1))
            v = run_cbmc()
            open(FILE, "w").write(orig)
            if v == "FAILED":
                killed += 1; tag = "KILLED"
            elif v == "SUCCESS":
                survived += 1; tag = "SURVIVED"
            else:
                tag = v
            results.append((i, tag, p.strip()))
            print(f"[{i:2}] {tag:9} {p.strip()}")
    finally:
        open(FILE, "w").write(orig)
    print(f"\nKILLED {killed} / {len(mutants)}  (survived {survived})")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
